openingMessage: Today we own our day before it owns us. Every crew out there is counting on the person next to them. Let's make sure we're worth counting on.
leadershipThought: Ownership isn't a title. It's what you do when no one's watching and the easy path is right in front of you.
opsContent:
Active Jobs (2026-06-14):
• SM Energy — First Tee Pad (Justin Pack): H2S readings at 3:00 PM — 113H 250ppm, 122H 600ppm, 112H 300ppm; 111H and 121H separators bypassed
• SM Energy — Gipple (Dennis Easley, 3:13 PM): 124H at 10,000ppm H2S; 154H at 2,500ppm H2S
• Exxon — Trailer 603 BM 3: Jordan and Joseph
• Exxon — Trailer 600 BM 10: Stefanie Kirschke and Edward C
• Texas ten disconnect: Jordan Lemmons heading out, coordinating well name/coordinates with Blake on arrival
• Williams Hazelwood: Complete — Joseph Popillion back to shop (2:44 PM)
• Pratt location (unassigned in visible frames): Waiting for pumper to shut other wells before hot work; no completion noted
• Shop — contingency/trailer readiness: Ray and Dawson (per Jimmy White, Jun 13 evening)

Carry-Forward:
• Exxon BM 10 tubing hole — confirm equalization complete and vac truck job closed out
• SM Energy Gipple 124H H2S (10,000ppm) — confirm safe levels or well status before next crew arrival
• First Tee Pad 111H and 121H separator bypass — reason and plan to restore needed
• Pratt hot work — confirm pumper arrived and wells shut; job status unknown
• Jordan Lemmons disconnect at Texas ten — coordinates sent to Blake; no completion note visible
